idea.hook: Want to visit the Milky Way's supermassive black hole?
facts: Our galaxy's central black hole, Sagittarius A*, is located ~26,000 light-years away.
Even traveling at the speed of light (300,000 km/s) would take 26,000 years to get there.
Our fastest spacecraft, the Parker Solar Probe, hit ~163 km/s. At that speed, reaching Sgr A* would take a staggering ~47.8 million years.
idea.payoff: Interstellar travel isn't just far, it's a journey spanning entire geological eras for us!
idea.title: To The Galactic Core?! 🚀🖤

scenes.text: Want to visit our galaxy's supermassive black hole?
scenes.visual: A visually stunning image of the Milky Way galaxy, subtly zooming in on its bright, glowing center.
An animated diagram showing the Milky Way, with Earth highlighted on one arm and an arrow pointing to a glowing 'X' at the galactic center, labeled with the distance.

scenes.text: At Light Speed (300,000 km/s): 26,000 YEARS to get there!
scenes.visual: A stylized beam of light streaks across a starfield, with a digital counter rapidly increasing, then settling on '26,000 Years'.

scenes.text: Our fastest craft (163 km/s)? 47.8 MILLION Years!
scenes.visual: An animation of the Parker Solar Probe flying at high speed, then a timeline graphic appearing, stretching incredibly far into the future, emphasizing millions of years.

scenes.text: Interstellar travel: a journey spanning entire geological eras!
scenes.visual: A time-lapse view of Earth's surface changing dramatically (e.g., continents shifting, mountains eroding), transitioning to a vast, ancient cosmic nebula, signifying immense time scales.

script.thumbnail_text: Galactic Travel Time: Millions of Years!
